ChangeRequest.name: AE Requirements - Distinction between collateral given and received
ChangeRequest.comment:
In many Asset Encumbrance templates information about collaterals is required and the requirements are always clearly differentiating between Collateral given and Collateral received. In particular:
* **Collateral received:** templates F 32.02.a/b * **Collateral given:** templates F 32.03.a/b
Behind both the categories of collateral, there are the type assets listed in F 32.01:
* Loans and advances * Debt Securities * Equities * Other (derivatives, Tangible assets, Intangible assets)
In case the collateral is an instrument (loans and advances, Equities that are instrument - green) in the model we have already the two different roles **Collateral Received/Collateral Given**.
However if for instance we have a collateral that is a security we can't directly distinguish if it is a collateral given or received and in order to cover AE we need to be able to do it.
Please find an extract from the model below:
![collateral rec_giv - SubView_1v1.png](/uploads/7419fda1181517795c41a84bd928a129/collateral_rec_giv_-_SubView_1v1.png)
A potential way to do it would be maybe to have a look to the financial role (asset/liability) of the item assigned to the collateral. For instance if the loan and advance instrument that is linked to the collateral via the `Loan (excluding repurchase agreement) and advance Collateral assignment` entity has the role of a financial asset we are probably talking about a collateral received while if it has the role of a liability we are talking about a collateral given. In any case we would need a sort of derivation to clearly assign to a specific asset, that is a collateral(yellow), the role of a collateral received/given.
Happy to discuss further!
